project: iClaw IM Bots Real China Channels
description: Turn the desktop IM Bots view from a static mock setup flow into a real China-channel integration surface backed by packaged OpenClaw plugins, persistent bot records, actual channel config mapping, and meaningful readiness checks.

userStories.title: Package all China IM plugins and the compatible official WeChat plugin into the runtime
userStories.description: As a release owner, I need the China IM plugin set bundled into the packaged OpenClaw runtime so that the desktop app ships with the full domestic channel surface instead of only the upstream built-ins.
acceptanceCriteria: Packaged plugin manifests include exactly these enabled plugin ids: claw-aegis, dingtalk, feishu-china, qqbot, wecom, wecom-app, wecom-kf, wechat-mp, openclaw-weixin
Official WeChat plugin is pinned to the legacy-compatible package line for OpenClaw 2026.3.13
Generated runtime config exposes plugins.entries for the same packaged plugin ids
Desktop packaged-plugin fixtures stay in sync with the source manifest
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expand the IM bot platform catalog to the full China channel set
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the IM bot setup catalog to list every packaged China channel with correct metadata so that the UI reflects the real channel advantage surface we now ship.
acceptanceCriteria: Desktop IM bot platform catalog includes these platform ids: dingtalk, feishu-china, qqbot, wecom, wecom-app, wecom-kf, wechat-mp, openclaw-weixin
Each platform entry defines plugin id, display label, guide URL, credential field schema, intro steps, and rollout notes
Legacy placeholder-only platform ids `feishu` and `qq` are removed from the IM bot catalog
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Show IM bots page as pure channel entry when no bots exist
userStories.description: As a first-time user, I want the empty IM bots page to focus on channel onboarding so that I am not distracted by an empty operations layout before any robot exists.
acceptanceCriteria: When IM bot count is 0, the page hides the `已创建的机器人` list and the right-side operations panel
When IM bot count is 0, the page still shows the platform access card area as the primary content
When IM bot count is greater than 0, the operations area becomes visible again without removing the platform access cards
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add a persisted IM bot record store for desktop
userStories.description: As a desktop app, I need IM bot records stored outside transient React state so that created bots survive reloads and can drive a real management surface.
acceptanceCriteria: Define a persisted IM bot record schema that stores platform id, credential payload, assistant binding, runtime state, and timestamps
Implement desktop-side load and save helpers for IM bot records using an existing durable storage location
Loading the IM bots page initializes from the persisted store instead of a hardcoded empty in-memory list
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Make the setup wizard create and update persisted IM bot records
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the setup wizard to create a real managed bot record so that the bot appears after reload and can be edited from the detail sheet.
acceptanceCriteria: Completing the setup wizard creates or updates a persisted IM bot record for the selected platform
Reloading the desktop app preserves previously created bots and their selected platform metadata
Editing the detail sheet saves back to the same persisted IM bot record
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Map persisted IM bot records into real OpenClaw channel configuration
userStories.description: As a runtime integrator, I need each managed IM bot to emit actual OpenClaw channel config so that packaged plugins can be configured by the desktop UI instead of only showing guide text.
acceptanceCriteria: Saving a bot record writes or updates channel config for the corresponding packaged plugin id in the desktop runtime config source
Per-platform field mapping covers dingtalk, feishu-china, qqbot, wecom, wecom-app, wecom-kf, wechat-mp, and openclaw-weixin
OpenClaw runtime config output after save contains the expected channel section for a created bot
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Replace fake IM connection tests with channel-aware readiness diagnostics
userStories.description: As an operator, I want the test action to diagnose the selected channel configuration and runtime prerequisites so that the setup flow tells me whether the robot is truly ready instead of only checking generic app health.
acceptanceCriteria: Connection test validates required credentials for the selected platform before reporting success
Connection test verifies packaged plugin presence and local OpenClaw runtime availability
Test results persist a platform-specific readiness summary onto the managed bot record
WeChat official plugin path surfaces QR-login-not-completed as a distinct readiness state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Support official WeChat QR-login guidance inside the IM bot setup flow
userStories.description: As a user configuring personal WeChat, I want the wizard to explain and expose the QR login step for the official plugin so that I can complete the only platform that needs interactive account authorization.
acceptanceCriteria: The `openclaw-weixin` setup flow clearly distinguishes QR login from static credential entry platforms
Wizard copy references the `openclaw channels login --channel openclaw-weixin` step in the correct place
The saved bot record can represent pending-login versus logged-in states for `openclaw-weixin`
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
